text:
There was an old man who had everything he ever wanted except a garden. He lived in an enormous house full of the finest things money could buy. But in the corner of his lavish bedroom, there was a barren spot on his window sill.

Every day, he would sit down and carefully plant a tiny seed. He named it Winston.

Winston didn't grow much, just slowly sprouted a single leaf. The man felt a deep connection with Winston as it grew and seemed to watch him.

Eventually, Winston bloomed into an unusual flower. It wasn't yellow or pink; instead, it looked like a small bird perched on a wire. It sang every evening.

Every night, the old man would look out his window and listen to Winston sing. It would serenade him with melodies of old songs, stories of long ago summers.

One summer night, a heavy storm blew in. Thunder rolled and rain poured. The old man thought Winston might be frightened.
